title: Weak Password Hashing
description: Use of MD5, SHA-1, or SHA-256 for password hashing violates SOC2 CC6.1. These algorithms are not designed for password storage and are vulnerable to brute-force and rainbow table attacks.

finding.message: Weak password hashing algorithm used at '{{token}}' in {{file}} at line {{line}}.

references: Use bcrypt, Argon2id, or scrypt for password hashing — these are designed to be slow and resistant to GPU attacks.
Migration path: on next successful login, re-hash the password with the new algorithm.
Never use general-purpose hash functions (MD5, SHA-*) for passwords; they are too fast.
Set work factors: bcrypt cost ≥ 12, Argon2id memory ≥ 64 MiB, iterations ≥ 3.
